rules.name: ACM Certificate Validation Hardening
rules.description: Detects ACM certificates using email validation or with certificate transparency logging disabled.
remediationSteps: Use DNS validation for automated certificate renewal
Enable certificate transparency logging to detect misissuance

rules.name: API Gateway Method Has No Authorization
rules.description: Detects AWS::ApiGateway::Method and AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Route resources where AuthorizationType is missing or NONE (excluding OPTIONS preflight). Public-by-default endpoints expose backend Lambdas to anyone on the internet.
remediationSteps: Set AuthorizationType to AWS_IAM, COGNITO_USER_POOLS, or CUSTOM (REST APIs)
Use JWT or AWS_IAM AuthorizationType for HTTP APIs (v2)
If the route is intentionally public (e.g. webhook receiver), prefer api-key + WAF rate limiting and suppress this rule explicitly

rules.name: API Gateway Publicly Accessible
rules.description: Detects API Gateway REST APIs configured with a non-PRIVATE endpoint, i.e. reachable from the public internet.
remediationSteps: Use a PRIVATE endpoint type with a VPC endpoint for internal APIs
For intentionally public APIs, attach an authorizer and a WAF web ACL
If public access is intended, suppress this rule for the resource

rules.name: API Gateway Default Endpoint Enabled With Custom Domain
rules.description: Detects REST APIs that leave the default execute-api endpoint enabled while a custom domain is configured, letting clients bypass the domain and its edge controls.
remediationSteps: Set DisableExecuteApiEndpoint to true on the REST API
Route all traffic through the custom domain and its WAF / edge controls

rules.name: API Gateway Stage Missing Throttling
rules.description: Detects API Gateway stages that configure no method-level rate or burst limits, leaving backends exposed to traffic spikes and uncontrolled cost.
remediationSteps: Add MethodSettings with ThrottlingRateLimit and ThrottlingBurstLimit (e.g. for HttpMethod "*")
Tune per-method limits for hot paths

rules.name: AppSync API Key Authentication
rules.description: Detects AppSync APIs using API key authentication or lacking additional authentication providers.
remediationSteps: Use AWS_IAM, Cognito User Pools, or OIDC for production workloads
Configure AdditionalAuthenticationProviders for client flexibility

rules.name: AppSync Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects AppSync APIs without logging, field-level logging, or X-Ray tracing.
remediationSteps: Configure LogConfig to capture request/response logs in CloudWatch
Set FieldLogLevel to ERROR or ALL for resolver execution detail
Enable XrayEnabled for distributed tracing

rules.name: AutoScaling LaunchConfiguration Assigns Public IP
rules.description: Detects AWS::AutoScaling::LaunchConfiguration with AssociatePublicIpAddress=true. Instances launched from this configuration get a public IP and are internet-reachable directly.
remediationSteps: Set AssociatePublicIpAddress to false on the LaunchConfiguration
Place instances in private subnets behind an ALB / NLB
Use a NAT Gateway / VPC endpoints for outbound traffic
Migrate to AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate (LaunchConfiguration is deprecated)

rules.name: AutoScaling Group Not Using ELB Health Checks
rules.description: Detects AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup resources attached to a load balancer (LoadBalancerNames or TargetGroupARNs) but using EC2 health checks. EC2 health checks miss app-level outages the load balancer would otherwise detect.
remediationSteps: Set HealthCheckType to ELB on the AutoScalingGroup
Set HealthCheckGracePeriod to give instances time to bootstrap before the LB starts probing
Verify the ALB / NLB target group has appropriate health-check thresholds

rules.name: Backup Plan Misconfigured
rules.description: Detects Backup plan rules without cross-region copy or lifecycle configuration.
remediationSteps: Add CopyActions to replicate backups cross-region for DR
Set DeleteAfterDays / MoveToColdStorageAfterDays to manage retention and cost

rules.name: Backup Vault Hardening
rules.description: Detects Backup vaults using AWS-managed keys or without a vault lock for compliance.
remediationSteps: Specify a customer-managed KMS key via EncryptionKeyArn
Add a BackupVaultLock to enforce retention and prevent deletion

rules.name: CloudFront HTTPS Only
rules.description: Detects CloudFront distributions allowing HTTP traffic.
remediationSteps: Set ViewerProtocolPolicy to https-only or redirect-to-https
Configure custom SSL certificate

rules.name: CloudFront Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects CloudFront distributions without access logging.
remediationSteps: Enable standard logging to S3
Consider real-time logs for detailed analysis

rules.name: CloudFront TLS Outdated
rules.description: Detects CloudFront distributions using outdated TLS versions.
remediationSteps: Set MinimumProtocolVersion to TLSv1.2_2021 or higher
Use a custom SSL certificate

rules.name: CloudFront WAF Missing
rules.description: Detects CloudFront distributions without WAF association.
remediationSteps: Associate AWS WAF web ACL with CloudFront distribution
Configure WAF rules for common attack patterns

rules.name: No CloudTrail Trail Defined
rules.description: Flags stacks that define no CloudTrail Trail. CloudTrail is usually configured once at the account or organization level — suppress this rule if account-level logging is already in place.
remediationSteps: Add an account- or organization-level CloudTrail Trail
Enable multi-region trails and log file validation
If account-level CloudTrail already exists, suppress this rule

rules.name: CloudWatch Alarm Actions Missing
rules.description: Detects CloudWatch Alarms without alarm actions or missing-data handling.
remediationSteps: Add AlarmActions (SNS, Lambda, SSM) for state-change notifications
Set TreatMissingData to define behaviour for missing data points

rules.name: CloudWatch Logs Retention Missing
rules.description: Detects CloudWatch Log Groups without an explicit retention period (logs retained indefinitely).
remediationSteps: Set RetentionInDays to control storage cost (30–90 days for ops, longer for compliance)

rules.name: CloudWatch Logs Encryption Disabled
rules.description: Detects CloudWatch Log Groups not encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key.
remediationSteps: Set KmsKeyId on the LogGroup to encrypt with a customer-managed key

rules.name: Cognito Password Policy Weak
rules.description: Detects Cognito user pools with weak password policies.
remediationSteps: Set minimum password length to at least 12 characters
Require uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols

rules.name: Cognito MFA Disabled
rules.description: Detects Cognito user pools without MFA enabled.
remediationSteps: Enable MFA (OPTIONAL or ON)
Configure SMS or TOTP as MFA method

rules.name: Cognito Advanced Security Disabled
rules.description: Detects Cognito user pools without advanced security features.
remediationSteps: Enable advanced security mode (AUDIT or ENFORCED)
Configure risk-based adaptive authentication

rules.name: Cross-Resource Misconfiguration
rules.description: Detects misconfigurations only visible across multiple resources: Lambda functions referencing VPC-only resources without VpcConfig, S3 replication without encryption configuration, and security groups exposing non-standard ports to the public when used by sensitive services.
remediationSteps: Configure VpcConfig on Lambda functions that reach VPC-only resources (RDS, ElastiCache, Redshift, OpenSearch, ECS Service)
Specify EncryptionConfiguration on S3 replication rules so replicated objects retain encryption at the destination
Restrict 0.0.0.0/0 ingress on non-standard ports for security groups attached to sensitive services

rules.name: EC2 IMDSv2 Not Enforced
rules.description: Detects launch templates and launch configurations that do not require IMDSv2 (MetadataOptions.HttpTokens is not "required"), leaving instances exposed to SSRF-based credential theft.
remediationSteps: Set MetadataOptions.HttpTokens to "required" on the launch template / launch configuration
In CDK, pass requireImdsv2: true on the instance or launch template

rules.name: AppSync WAF Missing
rules.description: Detects AppSync APIs without an associated AWS WAF WebACL.
remediationSteps: Associate a WAFv2 WebACL with the AppSync API
Define rules to protect against common web exploits

rules.name: Stack Architecture Recommendations
rules.description: Detects architectural omissions across the stack: missing CloudWatch Alarms on critical resources, Lambda functions without X-Ray tracing, public-facing resources without WAF, RDS databases without Secrets Manager rotation, and excessive NAT Gateway usage.
remediationSteps: Add CloudWatch Alarms on key metrics (Lambda errors, ECS task failures, RDS connections)
Enable X-Ray tracing on Lambda functions for distributed-request visibility
Associate AWS WAF with public-facing API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront distributions
Use Secrets Manager with automatic rotation for RDS database credentials
Audit NAT Gateway count and consider VPC endpoints or NAT instances for cost reduction

rules.name: DynamoDB Auto Scaling Missing
rules.description: Detects DynamoDB tables without auto-scaling enabled.
remediationSteps: Enable DynamoDB auto-scaling or use on-demand capacity
Set appropriate minimum and maximum capacity units

rules.name: DynamoDB Deletion Protection Disabled
rules.description: Detects DynamoDB tables without deletion protection, which can be destroyed by an accidental stack update or delete.
remediationSteps: Set DeletionProtectionEnabled to true on production tables
Use CloudFormation DeletionPolicy: Retain as defence in depth

rules.name: DynamoDB Table Uses AWS-Owned Encryption Key
rules.description: Detects AWS::DynamoDB::Table resources without SSESpecification.SSEEnabled=true. DynamoDB always encrypts at rest, but the default AWS-owned key cannot be inspected, audited, or rotated by the customer — inadequate for HIPAA / PCI-DSS / FedRAMP workloads.
remediationSteps: Set SSESpecification.SSEEnabled to true on the table
Provide SSESpecification.KMSMasterKeyId pointing at a customer-managed KMS key
Verify the KMS key has rotation enabled

rules.name: DynamoDB Point-in-Time Recovery Disabled
rules.description: Detects DynamoDB tables without Point-in-Time Recovery enabled. PITR provides continuous backups for the last 35 days and is required for SOC2 / HIPAA data resilience.
remediationSteps: Set PointInTimeRecoverySpecification.PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled to true on the table
Verify the AWS region supports PITR for your table type (Provisioned / On-Demand)

rules.name: DynamoDB Streams Disabled
rules.description: Detects DynamoDB tables without streams enabled.
remediationSteps: Enable DynamoDB Streams for change data capture
Consider point-in-time recovery for data protection

rules.name: EBS Volume Unencrypted
rules.description: Detects EBS volumes without encryption.
remediationSteps: Enable EBS encryption by default in account settings
Use KMS customer managed keys for additional control

rules.name: EC2 Instance Public IP
rules.description: Detects EC2 instances whose NetworkInterfaces specify AssociatePublicIpAddress=true, exposing the instance directly to the internet.
remediationSteps: Set AssociatePublicIpAddress to false on the NetworkInterface
Place the instance in a private subnet and use a NAT Gateway / VPC endpoint for outbound
Use an ALB / NLB in a public subnet to front the workload instead

rules.name: Security Group Unrestricted Ingress
rules.description: Detects security groups with unrestricted ingress (0.0.0.0/0).
remediationSteps: Restrict ingress to specific IP ranges
Use security group references for internal traffic
Implement least privilege network access

rules.name: EC2 Instance Type Outdated
rules.description: Detects EC2 instances using older generation instance types.
remediationSteps: Upgrade to current generation instance types
Use AWS Compute Optimizer for recommendations

rules.name: EC2 Subnet Auto-Assigns Public IPs
rules.description: Detects AWS::EC2::Subnet resources with MapPublicIpOnLaunch=true. Instances launched into the subnet inherit a public IP by default, which makes them internet-reachable without an explicit opt-in at the ENI level.
remediationSteps: Set MapPublicIpOnLaunch to false unless this is intentionally a public subnet
Have instances opt into a public IP via the ENI configuration when needed
Use VPC endpoints / NAT for outbound access from private subnets

rules.name: VPC Default Security Group Has Rules
rules.description: Detects default VPC security groups with ingress or egress rules; defaults should be empty.
remediationSteps: Remove all rules from the default security group
Use purpose-built security groups instead

rules.name: VPC Flow Logs Missing
rules.description: Detects VPCs without flow logs configured.
remediationSteps: Create an AWS::EC2::FlowLog resource targeting the VPC
Send logs to CloudWatch Logs or S3

rules.name: NAT Gateway Usage
rules.description: Identifies NAT Gateway usage for cost awareness.
remediationSteps: Consider VPC endpoints to reduce NAT Gateway traffic
Use NAT instances for lower traffic volumes

rules.name: ECR Scan-on-Push Disabled
rules.description: Detects ECR repositories without image vulnerability scanning on push.
remediationSteps: Enable ScanOnPush in ImageScanningConfiguration
Or use enhanced scanning at the registry level

rules.name: ECR Lifecycle Policy Missing
rules.description: Detects ECR repositories without a lifecycle policy to clean up old images.
remediationSteps: Define a LifecyclePolicy to expire untagged or excess image versions

rules.name: ECR Mutable Image Tags
rules.description: Detects ECR repositories that allow image tags to be overwritten.
remediationSteps: Set ImageTagMutability to IMMUTABLE

rules.name: ECS Container Running In Privileged Mode
rules.description: Detects ECS task definitions with a container running in privileged mode, which can access the host and escalate a container compromise into host compromise.
remediationSteps: Remove Privileged: true from the container definition unless strictly required
Grant only the specific Linux capabilities the workload needs via LinuxParameters

rules.name: ECS Secrets in Plaintext
rules.description: Detects ECS task definitions with sensitive data in plaintext environment variables.
remediationSteps: Use secrets property with Secrets Manager ARN
Use SSM Parameter Store SecureString

rules.name: ECS Deployment Circuit Breaker Disabled
rules.description: Detects ECS services (rolling-update controller) that do not enable the deployment circuit breaker, so a failed deployment is not stopped or rolled back automatically.
remediationSteps: Set DeploymentConfiguration.DeploymentCircuitBreaker.Enable to true
Set DeploymentConfiguration.DeploymentCircuitBreaker.Rollback to true so failed deployments revert to the last healthy task set

rules.name: ECS Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects ECS task definitions without logging configured.
remediationSteps: Configure awslogs log driver
Set up CloudWatch log groups with retention

rules.name: ECS Service Connect Access Logs Missing
rules.description: Detects ECS services with Service Connect enabled but no access log configuration, leaving inter-service traffic unaudited.
remediationSteps: Set ServiceConnectConfiguration.LogConfiguration with a LogDriver such as awslogs
Send Service Connect logs to a CloudWatch log group with a retention policy
Confirm log group encryption is configured for sensitive workloads

rules.name: ECS Resource Limits Missing
rules.description: Detects ECS task definitions without CPU/memory limits.
remediationSteps: Set CPU and memory at task level for Fargate
Set container-level memory limits for EC2

rules.name: ECS Task Definition Uses Mutable Image Tag
rules.description: Detects ECS task definition containers referencing an image by the mutable "latest" tag or no tag at all, so deployments are not reproducible and a changed upstream image can land without a code change.
remediationSteps: Reference container images by an immutable tag (release version or git SHA)
Or pin the image by @sha256 digest so every deployment runs the exact tested image

rules.name: EFS Encryption Disabled
rules.description: Detects AWS::EFS::FileSystem resources without Encrypted=true. The CFN default is unencrypted (unlike the EFS console default), and encryption can only be enabled at filesystem creation.
remediationSteps: Set Encrypted to true on the AWS::EFS::FileSystem resource
Provide KmsKeyId pointing at a customer-managed KMS key for additional control
For existing unencrypted filesystems, migrate data to a new encrypted filesystem

rules.name: EKS Public Endpoint Unrestricted
rules.description: Detects EKS clusters with the API endpoint reachable from 0.0.0.0/0 or only via the public network.
remediationSteps: Enable EndpointPrivateAccess for in-VPC node communication
Restrict PublicAccessCidrs to known administrative IP ranges

rules.name: EKS Secrets Encryption Disabled
rules.description: Detects EKS clusters without envelope encryption for Kubernetes secrets in etcd.
remediationSteps: Configure EncryptionConfig with a KMS key referencing resources: ["secrets"]

rules.name: EKS Control Plane Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects EKS clusters with no control plane log types enabled.
remediationSteps: Enable api, audit, authenticator, controllerManager, and scheduler logs

rules.name: ElastiCache Encryption Disabled
rules.description: Detects ElastiCache clusters without at-rest or in-transit encryption.
remediationSteps: Set AtRestEncryptionEnabled: true
Set TransitEncryptionEnabled: true

rules.name: ElastiCache AUTH Token Missing
rules.description: Detects Redis replication groups with transit encryption but no AUTH token configured.
remediationSteps: Set AuthToken to require Redis client authentication

rules.name: ElastiCache Automatic Failover Disabled
rules.description: Detects ElastiCache replication groups without automatic failover or Multi-AZ.
remediationSteps: Enable AutomaticFailoverEnabled
Enable MultiAZEnabled to span availability zones

rules.name: ELB HTTPS Listeners Missing
rules.description: Detects load balancers with HTTP listeners not redirecting to HTTPS.
remediationSteps: Add HTTPS listener with valid SSL certificate
Configure HTTP to HTTPS redirect

rules.name: ELB Deletion Protection Disabled
rules.description: Detects load balancers without deletion protection.
remediationSteps: Enable deletion protection for production load balancers
Use CloudFormation DeletionPolicy: Retain

rules.name: ELB Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects load balancers without access logging.
remediationSteps: Enable access logging to S3
Configure log retention policy

rules.name: ELB Security Policy Outdated
rules.description: Detects load balancers using outdated TLS security policies.
remediationSteps: Update to ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06 or newer
Remove support for deprecated protocols

rules.name: EventBridge Bus Policy Allows Wildcard Principal
rules.description: Detects AWS::Events::EventBusPolicy with an Allow statement targeting Principal=* (or AWS=*) without a Condition restricting access. Without the condition, any AWS account can publish events to the bus.
remediationSteps: Replace wildcard Principal with specific 12-digit AWS account IDs
Or scope the policy with a Condition such as aws:PrincipalOrgID
Verify the StatementId is descriptive — easier to audit later

rules.name: EventBridge Rule Disabled or Has No Targets
rules.description: Detects EventBridge rules that are disabled or have no targets configured, so matched events are silently dropped.
remediationSteps: Enable the rule (set State to ENABLED)
Attach at least one target to the rule

rules.name: Glue Connection Network Isolation
rules.description: Detects Glue JDBC connections without VPC subnet placement or SSL enforcement.
remediationSteps: Set PhysicalConnectionRequirements with SubnetId and SecurityGroupIdList
Include SSL parameters in the JDBC connection URL

rules.name: Glue Job Encryption Missing
rules.description: Detects Glue jobs without an attached SecurityConfiguration for encrypting bookmarks, S3 data, and CloudWatch logs.
remediationSteps: Create a SecurityConfiguration enabling KMS encryption for all targets
Reference it via the job's SecurityConfiguration property

rules.name: IAM Overly Permissive Policies
rules.description: Detects IAM policies with overly permissive actions like * wildcards.
remediationSteps: Review IAM policies for overly broad permissions
Replace wildcard (*) actions with specific actions
Use IAM Access Analyzer to identify unused permissions

rules.name: IAM Cross-Account Trust
rules.description: Detects IAM roles with cross-account trust relationships.
remediationSteps: Verify cross-account trust is intentional
Add conditions to restrict access (e.g., external ID)
Document the business justification

rules.name: IAM User Direct Policies
rules.description: Detects IAM Users with policies attached directly (inline Policies or ManagedPolicyArns). CIS AWS Foundations 1.15 requires users to inherit permissions through groups.
remediationSteps: Create an IAM Group with the required policies
Add the user to the group instead of attaching policies directly
Remove Policies and ManagedPolicyArns from AWS::IAM::User resources

rules.name: IAM Permission Boundary Missing
rules.description: Detects IAM roles without permission boundaries.
remediationSteps: Create a permission boundary policy
Attach the permission boundary to IAM roles

rules.name: Kinesis Encryption Disabled
rules.description: Detects Kinesis Data Streams without server-side KMS encryption.
remediationSteps: Set StreamEncryption with EncryptionType: KMS and a KeyId

rules.name: Kinesis Minimum Retention
rules.description: Detects Kinesis Data Streams using the default 24-hour retention period.
remediationSteps: Increase RetentionPeriodHours to support replay/reprocessing during incidents

rules.name: KMS Key Policy Allows Public Access
rules.description: Detects KMS keys whose key policy grants access to a wildcard or public principal.
remediationSteps: Remove wildcard/public principals from the key policy
Scope key access to specific IAM principals or accounts

rules.name: KMS Key Policy Self-Lockout
rules.description: Detects AWS::KMS::Key resources whose KeyPolicy contains a Deny statement on kms:*, kms:PutKeyPolicy, or * with a broad Principal and no carveout for the account root or admin role. Such a policy locks the account out of its own key — recovery requires breaking glass on the account root credentials AND scheduling a 7-30 day key deletion (which does not actually let you recover the key, just remove it). Effectively unrecoverable for any data the key has encrypted; AWS Support typically declines to intervene where customers have a self-service path via root.
remediationSteps: Keep the auto-injected root Allow statement (CDK's defaultKeyPolicy) intact, or add an explicit Allow on Principal: { AWS: 'arn:aws:iam::<account>:root' } for Action: kms:*
Or add a NotPrincipal exemption for the account root or admin role(s)
Or use a Condition with StringNotEquals on aws:PrincipalArn to exempt admin role ARNs

rules.name: Lambda Permission Permits Public Access
rules.description: Detects AWS::Lambda::Permission and AWS::Lambda::LayerVersionPermission resources that grant invoke / use rights with a wildcard Principal, or grant a service principal without a SourceArn / SourceAccount / PrincipalOrgID restriction (confused-deputy risk).
remediationSteps: Replace wildcard Principal with a specific 12-digit AWS account ID or {Ref: AWS::AccountId}
For service principals, set SourceArn to the specific resource that may invoke the function
For S3 service principal, set SourceAccount to your AWS account ID
For LayerVersionPermission, prefer OrganizationId over wildcard Principal

rules.name: Lambda Sensitive Environment Variables
rules.description: Detects Lambda functions with sensitive data in environment variables.
remediationSteps: Move sensitive data to AWS Secrets Manager
Use SSM Parameter Store with SecureString
Enable Lambda environment variable encryption

rules.name: Lambda Deprecated Runtime
rules.description: Detects Lambda functions using runtimes that have reached or are approaching end-of-life.
remediationSteps: Upgrade to a currently supported runtime
Track AWS Lambda runtime deprecation announcements

rules.name: Lambda Function URL Without Authentication
rules.description: Detects Lambda Function URLs configured with AuthType NONE, which allows unauthenticated public invocation.
remediationSteps: Set AuthType to AWS_IAM so callers are authorized with IAM SigV4
Or front the function with an authenticated API Gateway / CloudFront + WAF

rules.name: Lambda Memory Optimization
rules.description: Detects Lambda functions with suboptimal memory configuration.
remediationSteps: Use AWS Lambda Power Tuning to find optimal memory
Consider cost vs performance tradeoffs

rules.name: Lambda Variable Runtime
rules.description: Detects Lambda functions using a variable runtime (Runtime.NODEJS_LATEST or useLatestRuntimeVersion) that resolves to the newest runtime at synth time, causing silent in-place runtime upgrades on the next deploy after a CDK bump.
remediationSteps: Pin an explicit runtime (e.g. Runtime.NODEJS_22_X)
Or set useLatestRuntimeVersion: false on NodejsFunction
Treat runtime upgrades as a deliberate, reviewed change

rules.name: Lambda VPC NAT Gateway Cost Warning
rules.description: Warns about potential NAT Gateway costs for VPC-attached Lambda functions.
remediationSteps: Use VPC endpoints instead of NAT Gateway where possible
Consider if VPC attachment is necessary
Monitor NAT Gateway data processing costs

rules.name: Lambda Dead Letter Queue Missing
rules.description: Detects Lambda functions without dead letter queue configuration.
remediationSteps: Configure a dead letter queue (SQS or SNS)
Set up monitoring for DLQ messages
Implement retry logic for failed invocations

rules.name: Lambda Reserved Concurrency Missing
rules.description: Detects Lambda functions without reserved concurrency.
remediationSteps: Set reserved concurrency for critical functions
Use provisioned concurrency for latency-sensitive workloads

rules.name: Lambda X-Ray Tracing Disabled
rules.description: Detects Lambda functions without active X-Ray tracing, reducing observability into latency and errors.
remediationSteps: Set TracingConfig.Mode to Active on the function
Grant the function the AWSXRayDaemonWriteAccess managed policy

rules.name: MSK Client Authentication Missing
rules.description: Detects MSK clusters without client authentication or that allow unauthenticated access.
remediationSteps: Enable SASL/SCRAM, IAM auth, or mTLS in ClientAuthentication
Disable Unauthenticated access

rules.name: MSK Encryption Weak
rules.description: Detects MSK clusters without TLS for client-broker traffic, without inter-broker encryption, or using AWS-managed keys for at-rest encryption.
remediationSteps: Set ClientBroker to TLS in EncryptionInTransit
Set InCluster to true in EncryptionInTransit
Specify DataVolumeKMSKeyId for at-rest encryption

rules.name: MSK Broker Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects MSK clusters without broker logs configured.
remediationSteps: Enable broker logs to CloudWatch Logs, Firehose, or S3

rules.name: OpenSearch Encryption Disabled
rules.description: Detects OpenSearch domains without at-rest encryption or node-to-node encryption.
remediationSteps: Enable EncryptionAtRestOptions with a KMS key
Enable NodeToNodeEncryptionOptions

rules.name: OpenSearch Access Control Weak
rules.description: Detects OpenSearch domains without fine-grained access control or VPC placement.
remediationSteps: Enable AdvancedSecurityOptions
Configure VPCOptions to deploy the domain inside a VPC

rules.name: OpenSearch Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects OpenSearch domains without audit logs or application logs published to CloudWatch.
remediationSteps: Enable AUDIT_LOGS in LogPublishingOptions
Enable ES_APPLICATION_LOGS in LogPublishingOptions

rules.name: RDS Encryption Disabled
rules.description: Detects RDS instances without encryption at rest.
remediationSteps: Enable encryption for new RDS instances
For existing instances, create encrypted snapshot and restore

rules.name: RDS Publicly Accessible
rules.description: Detects RDS instances that are publicly accessible.
remediationSteps: Set PubliclyAccessible to false
Use VPC security groups to control access
Access databases through bastion hosts or VPN

rules.name: RDS Backup Retention Low
rules.description: Detects RDS instances with insufficient backup retention.
remediationSteps: Set backup retention period to at least 7 days
Consider longer retention for compliance requirements
Enable cross-region backup replication

rules.name: RDS Deletion Protection Disabled
rules.description: Detects RDS instances without deletion protection.
remediationSteps: Enable deletion protection for production databases
Use CloudFormation DeletionPolicy: Retain

rules.name: RDS Enhanced Monitoring Disabled
rules.description: Detects RDS instances without enhanced monitoring (MonitoringInterval missing or set to 0). OS-level metrics are not published to CloudWatch Logs.
remediationSteps: Set MonitoringInterval to one of 1, 5, 10, 15, 30, or 60 (seconds)
Provide MonitoringRoleArn pointing at an IAM role with AmazonRDSEnhancedMonitoringRole attached

rules.name: RDS Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects RDS DBInstance and DBCluster resources without EnableCloudwatchLogsExports configured. Engine logs (error, slow query, audit) are not exported to CloudWatch for retention or analysis.
remediationSteps: Set EnableCloudwatchLogsExports to the engine's relevant log types (e.g. ['error','slowquery'] for MySQL, ['postgresql'] for PostgreSQL)
Configure a CloudWatch Logs retention policy on the resulting log group
For Aurora, enable both DBCluster and per-instance log exports

rules.name: RDS Managed Master Secret Without Customer-Managed Key
rules.description: Detects RDS instances/clusters using the native Secrets Manager integration (ManageMasterUserPassword) whose managed master-user secret is encrypted with the AWS-managed aws/secretsmanager key instead of a customer-managed KMS key.
remediationSteps: Set MasterUserSecret.KmsKeyId to a customer-managed KMS key
Apply a key policy that scopes who can decrypt the database credential secret
Enable key rotation and CloudTrail auditing on the customer-managed key

rules.name: RDS Instance Using gp2 Storage
rules.description: Detects Multi-AZ RDS instances on gp2 storage; gp3 offers better baseline performance at lower cost.
remediationSteps: Migrate the instance storage type from gp2 to gp3
Right-size provisioned IOPS/throughput for gp3

rules.name: Redshift Publicly Accessible
rules.description: Detects Redshift clusters reachable from the public internet.
remediationSteps: Set PubliclyAccessible: false
Use VPC endpoints, bastion hosts, or VPN for access

rules.name: Redshift Encryption Disabled
rules.description: Detects Redshift clusters without at-rest encryption.
remediationSteps: Set Encrypted: true and specify a KmsKeyId

rules.name: Redshift Audit Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects Redshift clusters without audit logging to S3 (connection, user, query activity).
remediationSteps: Set LoggingProperties with a target S3 bucket and key prefix

rules.name: Route 53 DNSSEC Disabled
rules.description: Detects Route 53 hosted zones without DNSSEC signing enabled.
remediationSteps: Create an AWS::Route53::DNSSEC resource for the hosted zone
Configure a key-signing key (KSK)

rules.name: Route 53 Health Check Suboptimal
rules.description: Detects Route 53 health checks using HTTP instead of HTTPS or the standard request interval.
remediationSteps: Use HTTPS to validate certificate and endpoint security
Use a 10-second request interval for critical endpoints

rules.name: Route 53 Query Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects Route 53 hosted zones without query logging.
remediationSteps: Create an AWS::Route53::QueryLoggingConfig pointing to a CloudWatch Logs group

rules.name: S3 BucketPolicy Self-Lockout
rules.description: Detects AWS::S3::BucketPolicy resources containing a Deny statement on s3:DeleteBucket, s3:*, or * with a broad Principal and no NotPrincipal or aws:PrincipalArn carveout for the account root or admin role. Such a policy locks the account out of its own bucket — recovery requires breaking glass on the account root credentials, since AWS Support typically declines to intervene where customers have a self-service path via root.
remediationSteps: Add a NotPrincipal exemption for the account root (arn:aws:iam::<account>:root) or admin role(s)
Or use a Condition with StringNotEquals on aws:PrincipalArn to exempt admin role ARNs
If the Deny is intended for transport security, scope it with aws:SecureTransport rather than a blanket Deny on Principal: '*'

rules.name: S3 Bucket Public Access
rules.description: Detects S3 buckets with public access enabled.
remediationSteps: Enable S3 Block Public Access settings
Review bucket policies for public access
Use VPC endpoints for private access

rules.name: S3 BucketPolicy Permits Non-SSL Requests
rules.description: Detects AWS::S3::BucketPolicy resources that lack a Deny statement enforcing aws:SecureTransport. Without this, the bucket accepts plain HTTP requests in addition to HTTPS.
remediationSteps: Add a Deny statement with Action s3:* and Condition Bool aws:SecureTransport=false
Apply the Deny to both the bucket ARN and the bucket-objects ARN (arn/* )
Use BoolIfExists if the policy already accepts unsigned requests via specific clauses

rules.name: S3 Bucket Access Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects S3 buckets without server access logging configured. Required by CIS AWS Foundations 2.1.2 and useful for incident forensics.
remediationSteps: Set LoggingConfiguration.DestinationBucketName on the bucket to capture access logs
Use a separate bucket for logs to avoid recursive logging
Configure a lifecycle rule on the log bucket to manage storage costs

rules.name: S3 Bucket Versioning Disabled
rules.description: Detects S3 buckets without versioning enabled, leaving overwritten or deleted objects unrecoverable.
remediationSteps: Set VersioningConfiguration.Status to Enabled on the bucket
Add lifecycle rules to expire noncurrent versions and control cost

rules.name: S3 Lifecycle Policy Missing
rules.description: Detects S3 buckets without lifecycle policies.
remediationSteps: Create lifecycle rules to transition objects to cheaper storage classes
Set expiration rules for temporary data
Use lifecycle rules to delete incomplete multipart uploads

rules.name: S3 Intelligent Tiering
rules.description: Recommends S3 Intelligent Tiering for cost optimization.
remediationSteps: Enable S3 Intelligent-Tiering for buckets with varying access patterns
Configure archive access tiers for infrequently accessed data

rules.name: S3 Replication Missing
rules.description: Detects critical S3 buckets without cross-region replication.
remediationSteps: Enable cross-region replication for disaster recovery
Configure replication rules for critical data

rules.name: Secrets Manager Secret Publicly Accessible
rules.description: Detects Secrets Manager secrets whose resource policy grants access to a wildcard or public principal.
remediationSteps: Remove wildcard/public principals from the secret resource policy
Scope access to specific IAM principals or accounts

rules.name: SNS TopicPolicy Self-Lockout
rules.description: Detects AWS::SNS::TopicPolicy resources containing a Deny statement on sns:DeleteTopic, sns:RemovePermission, sns:*, or * with a broad Principal and no NotPrincipal or aws:PrincipalArn carveout for the account root or admin role. Such a policy locks the account out of its own topic — recovery requires breaking glass on the account root credentials to remove the topic policy, or delete-and-recreate the topic if no subscribers depend on its ARN. In fan-out architectures that is often a multi-stack rebuild.
remediationSteps: Add a NotPrincipal exemption for the account root (arn:aws:iam::<account>:root) or admin role(s)
Or use a Condition with StringNotEquals on aws:PrincipalArn to exempt admin role ARNs
If the Deny is intended to scope to AWS services, use aws:PrincipalIsAWSService rather than a blanket Deny on Principal: '*'

rules.name: SNS Encryption Disabled
rules.description: Detects SNS topics without encryption.
remediationSteps: Enable server-side encryption with KMS
Use customer managed keys for additional control

rules.name: SQS QueuePolicy Self-Lockout
rules.description: Detects AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy resources containing a Deny statement on sqs:DeleteQueue, sqs:RemovePermission, sqs:*, or * with a broad Principal and no NotPrincipal or aws:PrincipalArn carveout for the account root or admin role. Such a policy locks the account out of its own queue — recovery requires breaking glass on the account root credentials to remove the queue policy, or delete-and-recreate the queue if nothing else depends on its ARN. In event-driven systems that is often a multi-stack rebuild.
remediationSteps: Add a NotPrincipal exemption for the account root (arn:aws:iam::<account>:root) or admin role(s)
Or use a Condition with StringNotEquals on aws:PrincipalArn to exempt admin role ARNs
If the Deny is intended to scope to AWS services, use aws:PrincipalIsAWSService rather than a blanket Deny on Principal: '*'

rules.name: SQS Encryption Disabled
rules.description: Detects SQS queues without encryption.
remediationSteps: Enable server-side encryption with KMS
Use SQS-managed keys (SSE-SQS) or customer managed keys

rules.name: SQS Queue Without Dead-Letter Queue
rules.description: Detects SQS queues with no RedrivePolicy, so messages that repeatedly fail processing are lost instead of captured for inspection.
remediationSteps: Add a RedrivePolicy pointing at a dead-letter queue with a sensible maxReceiveCount
Alarm on the dead-letter queue depth so poison messages are noticed

rules.name: Step Functions Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects Step Functions state machines without logging.
remediationSteps: Enable CloudWatch Logs for state machine execution
Set appropriate log level (ALL, ERROR, FATAL, OFF)

rules.name: WAF Logging Disabled
rules.description: Detects WAFv2 WebACLs without logging configuration for analysed requests.
remediationSteps: Create an AWS::WAFv2::LoggingConfiguration for the WebACL

rules.name: WAF WebACL Misconfigured
rules.description: Detects WAFv2 WebACLs with no rules, default Allow action, or CloudWatch metrics disabled.
remediationSteps: Add WAF rules covering the OWASP Top 10 baseline
Set DefaultAction to Block for defence-in-depth
Enable CloudWatchMetricsEnabled in VisibilityConfig
